jD8MDo + siblings — how they fill the screen (from CSSReferencefile.txt)
================================================================================

CONTEXT
-------
.jD8MDo lives under parent .n1I9fl, which is a horizontal flex row
(display: flex, default flex-direction: row) with width: 100% of .VYqscx.
.n1I9fl has height: 90rem and padding-top / padding-bottom: 11.25rem each,
so the inner content band height is 67.5rem (matches .mUiHp_ height).


SIBLINGS IN THE SAME ROW (selectors under .n1I9fl)
--------------------------------------------------
1. .jD8MDo
   - width: 31.25rem (base / flex-basis before grow)
   - flex-grow: 10, flex-shrink: 10
   - height: 100%
   - background: repeat-x, background-size: auto 100%

2. .mUiHp_
   - flex-grow: 0, flex-shrink: 0  → does NOT take extra space
   - width: 120rem (fixed in the flex sense)
   - height: 67.5rem

3. .TXNnjm
   - Same flex pattern as .jD8MDo but base width: 8.75rem
   - flex-grow: 10, flex-shrink: 10, height: 100%, similar background


HOW .jD8MDo FILLS HORIZONTAL SPACE WITH SIBLINGS
------------------------------------------------
- Main axis = row. Minimum content width is roughly:
  31.25rem + 120rem + 8.75rem (plus any gaps from markup).

- .mUiHp_ never grows (flex-grow: 0), so ALL leftover horizontal space in
  the row is distributed to flex items that CAN grow.

- .jD8MDo and .TXNnjm both have flex-grow: 10, so they SHARE leftover width
  in a 1:1 ratio. Their declared widths are starting sizes; flex-grow is what
  stretches them so the row still fills the full width of .n1I9fl.

- flex-shrink: 10 lets those same columns shrink if the row is too narrow.


VERTICAL FILL
-------------
- .jD8MDo uses height: 100% so it spans the flex container’s content box
  height (inside the padding), same idea as .TXNnjm.

- .mUiHp_ uses an explicit 67.5rem height aligned with that inner band.


VISUAL “FULL BLEED” BACKGROUND ON .jD8MDo
------------------------------------------
- background-repeat: repeat-x and background-size: auto 100% tile the image
  horizontally and scale it to the element’s height, so when .jD8MDo grows
  wider, the strip still looks filled.


ONE-LINE SUMMARY
----------------
.n1I9fl is full-width flex row; .mUiHp_ is a fixed 120rem stage; .jD8MDo and
.TXNnjm use flex-grow: 10 to split extra horizontal space and height: 100% to
fill the vertical band — together they fill the screen area that row occupies.

Reference: Resize-scaling-notes/CSSReferencefile.txt (lines ~11–47)
